title: Semantic Kernel Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Semantic Kernel with API and CLI commands.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content like poetry or storytelling.

commands.description: Specifies the AI model version to use.
commands.scenario: When you need specific features or capabilities of a certain model version.

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in the response.
commands.scenario: When you want to control the length of generated text to save costs.

commands.description: Enables streaming of responses for real-time applications.
commands.scenario: When building a chat application that requires immediate response display.

commands.description: Instructs the model to break down code explanations into steps.
commands.scenario: When teaching programming concepts or debugging complex code.

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating the same line verbatim.
commands.scenario: When generating text that should be diverse and non-repetitive.

commands.description: Outputs the result in JSON format for easy parsing.
commands.scenario: When integrating the tool with other systems that require JSON input.

commands.description: Prompts the model to generate a summary of the given text.
commands.scenario: When you need a concise version of a long document.

commands.description: Encourages the model to introduce new topics in the response.
commands.scenario: When you want the model to explore new ideas or topics.

commands.description: Provides detailed logs of the operations being performed.
commands.scenario: When debugging issues or understanding the tool's behavior in detail.

commands.description: Instructs the model to translate text into Spanish.
commands.scenario: When building a multilingual application or service.

commands.description: Defines sequences where the model should stop generating further text.
commands.scenario: When you want to control where the output should end, such as after a sentence.

commands.command: "List the pros and cons of using AI in healthcare."
commands.description: Prompts the model to generate a balanced view on a topic.
commands.scenario: When writing articles or reports that require a balanced perspective.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for the operation to complete.
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure the operation completes within a certain time frame.

commands.description: Implement a custom filter to intercept and modify function arguments or results globally before/after execution for centralized logging or validation.
Integrate vector databases (e.g., Qdrant, Pinecone) using the KernelMemory abstraction to enable RAG-based long-term memory retrieval.
Enable 'Function Choice Behavior' to allow the LLM to automatically select and execute multiple registered plugins in a single turn.
Use strongly-typed argument binding to map complex object properties directly to function parameters, reducing manual dictionary manipulation.
Attach an OpenTelemetry-compliant exporter to the Kernel to trace the exact execution flow and latency of individual plugin steps in distributed systems.
Instantly registers a C# class as a plugin by scanning for [KernelFunction] attributes, enabling seamless tool integration.
Configures the LLM to dynamically decide which functions to call during a single invocation, enabling complex multi-step reasoning.
Dynamically injects runtime context or variables into a prompt template without modifying the underlying template string.
Advanced parameter to control whether the model returns tool calls immediately or waits for the kernel to execute them automatically.
Initializes a specialized vector database connector (like Qdrant or Chroma) to enable RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) workflows.
